concepts.name: Fuzzy company-name matching
concepts.summary: Match messy company names across CRMs using Unicode NFKD normalization followed by Levenshtein edit distance with a threshold of 3.
reasoning: Chose Levenshtein distance over Jaro-Winkler because it handled the observed typo cases more consistently.
Rejected naive substring matching because it fails on accented (Unicode) characters.
Rejected trigram/n-gram similarity because names are short (1-2 tokens), making trigram overlap noisy.
Normalize input with Unicode NFKD and strip punctuation before computing Levenshtein distance.
